label: SES-057 Code Change Lifecycle workstream established: planning_items resolution gap diagnosed, Option III adopted, kickoff authored; 1 session, 3 decisions (DEC-168..170), 7 planning items (PI-027..033), 10 references

session.title: Code Change Lifecycle workstream established: diagnosed planning_items resolution gap, adopted first-class commit entities (Option III), authored kickoff for methodology drafting conversation
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai diagnostic-and-design conversation. Opened on the question of why only one planning_item shows status Resolved in the CRMBUILDER engagement database despite many completed workstreams. Produced a diagnostic script (tools/diagnostics/diagnose_planning_items.py, commit dde4eb4) and its companion Claude Code investigation prompt (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-diagnose-planning-items.md, commit 77010bf), then analyzed Claude Code's structured findings to identify three concrete gaps in the governance lifecycle coverage. Doug adopted Option III over Options I and II — first-class commit entities, methodology-first sequencing — and confirmed the seven-planning-item workstream decomposition and the tight kickoff document style. The kickoff document is structurally a work_ticket (kickoff_prompt kind, ready status) but is not authored as a WT-NNN database record in this close-out — close-out payloads do not currently support a work_tickets section. Back-fill via PI-033 will retroactively author it once PI-030 ships the payload format extension.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Why is there only one planning item with a status of resolved in the crmbuilder V2 database. We have completed a number of steps, so I would expect there to be completed records in the planning items database?"

Structured summary of the diagnostic-and-design work:

(1) Initial hypothesis framing. Proposed two likely causes for the under-resolution: a close-out discipline gap (the apply flow never transitions prior planning_items) or a vocabulary gap (no resolution-style relationship_kind exists). Authored a diagnostic script and committed it to tools/diagnostics/diagnose_planning_items.py (commit dde4eb4) after Doug picked option 1 (use existing tools/diagnostics/ directory) over option 2 (new automation/diagnostics/) on the placement question. Authored a Claude Code investigation prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-diagnose-planning-items.md (commit 77010bf) for Doug to run.

(2) Diagnostic execution. Claude Code ran the prompt against ~/Dropbox/Projects/crmbuilder/crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db. Status distribution: 25 Open, 1 Resolved (PI-021), total 26. The one Resolved record is a same-workstream same-day create-and-resolve case: PI-021 created in SES-044 on 2026-05-19, status flipped to Resolved on 2026-05-20 with no companion edge and resolution_reference left NULL. The planning_items.resolution_reference column exists as VARCHAR(64) — clear past design intent — but is NULL on every row including PI-021. The refs vocabulary has 17 relationship_kinds and zero resolution-style kinds for planning_items; decisions get decided_in (163 rows, a true resolution edge), planning_items get only is_about (27 rows, overloaded) and blocks (2 rows). Three diagnostic script bugs identified: table is named refs not references; the PRAGMA call was unquoted; the status filter status != 'open' is case-sensitive against title-case values Open and Resolved.

(3) Three concrete gaps surfaced. Gap A — work_tickets are not being authored when Claude Code prompts get committed: the work_ticket entity is fully defined (governance-schema-specs/work_ticket.md, status lifecycle drafted to ready to consumed) and explicitly cites CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md files as canonical examples, but no Claude Code prompts authored after the v0.7 backfill have been recorded as work_ticket database rows. Gap B — no resolves relationship_kind for planning_items; resolution_reference column NULL on every row. Gap C — git commits are not first-class; they live only as text in session.artifacts_produced.

(4) Options framed at three scope levels. Option I (resolves only) — cheapest, does not satisfy the audit goal. Option II (wire existing entities plus add resolution) — middle scope, uses the existing work_ticket entity to make the planning_item to work_ticket to conversation to close_out_payload to deposit_event to planning_item-resolved chain machine-readable end-to-end without modeling git commits. Option III (Option II plus first-class commits) — comprehensive, adds the commit entity and git-log ingestion to make the planning_item to commit-SHA chain queryable in a single SQL join.

(5) Decisions. DEC-168 adopt Option III on Doug's rationale that integration burden grows with delay. DEC-169 methodology-first sequencing — methodology document drives schema additions, not vice versa. DEC-170 seven-planning-item workstream decomposition.

(6) Deferred decisions list named for the methodology conversation (PI-027): commit identifier strategy (CM-NNN sequence vs SHA-as-identifier); granularity of resolves (per-commit vs per-conversation vs both); commit ingestion trigger (manual command vs git post-commit hook vs background polling); commit record fields (sha, message, author, committed_at, repository, branch, parent_sha, files_changed_count, plus any others); multi-repo scoping (commits to ClevelandBusinessMentoring repo vs commits to crmbuilder repo across engagement DBs); typed addresses relationship_kind for work_ticket to planning_item versus stay with generic is_about; work_ticket authoring rule scope (only CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md or all kickoff_prompt-kind documents). Also includes a settle-the-direction call on the blocks relationship_kind, which the v0.7 backfill used in a direction the diagnostic could not unambiguously interpret.

(7) Diagnostic script bugs folded into PI-033 back-fill rather than fixed in isolation, since the script will need re-running against the new schema after PI-029 and PI-030 land anyway.

(8) Kickoff document authored at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/code-change-lifecycle-workstream-establishing-kickoff.md in tight format — goal, deferred decisions, deliverable shape, read list. Full diagnostic walkthrough kept in this session record rather than duplicated into the kickoff. Cannot be authored as a WT-NNN record in this close-out because close-out payloads do not yet support a work_tickets section.
session.artifacts_produced: tools/diagnostics/diagnose_planning_items.py (commit dde4eb4, this conversation); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-diagnose-planning-items.md (commit 77010bf, this conversation); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/code-change-lifecycle-workstream-establishing-kickoff.md (this conversation); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_057.json (this payload); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-057.md (apply prompt); three decisions DEC-168 DEC-169 DEC-170; seven planning items PI-027 through PI-033; ten references (three decided_in plus seven is_about).
session.in_flight_at_end: Code Change Lifecycle workstream queued for execution as seven sequential planning items. PI-027 (methodology document) is the next conversation, opening against the kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/code-change-lifecycle-workstream-establishing-kickoff.md. The methodology conversation must settle seven deferred design decisions (commit identifier strategy, granularity of resolves, ingestion trigger, commit record fields, multi-repo scoping, typed addresses kind, work_ticket authoring rule scope) plus the blocks direction question before the schema spec for the commit entity (PI-028) can begin. After all seven planning items resolve, the V2 governance system supports the full planning_item to commit-SHA audit chain as data: every Claude Code prompt is a work_ticket record, every commit is a commit record, every resolution is an edge from session to planning_item with the resolution_reference column populated. The kickoff document is structurally a work_ticket (kickoff_prompt kind, status ready) but is not authored as a WT-NNN database record here because close-out payloads do not yet support a work_tickets section; PI-033 back-fill will retroactively author it once PI-030 ships the payload format extension. Diagnostic script bugs (refs vs references; unquoted PRAGMA; case-sensitive status filter) folded into PI-033 rather than fixed in isolation.

decisions.title: Adopt Option III for the Code Change Lifecycle workstream — first-class commit entities, full planning_item-to-commit-SHA audit chain as data
decisions.context: The diagnostic script run against CRMBUILDER.db revealed that only one of 26 planning_items shows status Resolved, with the resolution achieved via a manual status flip with no companion governance edge. Three gaps surface: work_tickets are not being authored when Claude Code prompts get committed (Gap A), no resolves relationship_kind exists for planning_items and the resolution_reference column is NULL on every row (Gap B), and git commits are not first-class entities — they live only as text in session.artifacts_produced (Gap C). Three options were framed at increasing scope: Option I closes Gap B only (resolves edge plus resolution_reference population); Option II closes Gaps A and B (resolves plus work_ticket authoring methodology, using the existing work_ticket entity); Option III closes all three (Option II plus first-class commits with git-log ingestion).
decisions.decision: Adopt Option III. The Code Change Lifecycle workstream will add the commit entity to V2 as a first-class governance record, add resolves relationship_kinds, build git-log ingestion, and update methodology to author work_ticket records for every committed Claude Code prompt. The planning_item-to-commit-SHA audit chain becomes machine-readable end-to-end via a single SQL join across planning_items, refs, sessions, work_tickets, conversations, close_out_payloads, deposit_events, and commits.
decisions.rationale: Doug's explicit rationale: integration burden grows with delay. Every new Claude Code prompt committed without a work_ticket record extends the back-fill burden; every conversation closing without a resolves mechanism leaves more planning_items in indeterminate state. The audit-trail use case (defect investigation traceback from a code change to the planning item that motivated it, used in 2027 to investigate a regression in a feature that shipped in 2026) is not satisfied by free-text artifacts_produced — the prose is read-once. Option I closes the narrow gap (status flips work) but does not give the trace from intent to delivery. Option II covers most of the chain but stops at the conversation entity, requiring out-of-band tooling to walk from a conversation record to its commits. Option III completes the chain so that future defect investigation in the V2 system is a single SQL query against governance data rather than a tool chain across the database, git log, and prose fields.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option I — resolves relationship_kind only, populate resolution_reference column, leave Gap A and Gap C unaddressed. Cheapest but does not satisfy the audit-trail goal that Doug named as the actual requirement. Option II — Option I plus work_ticket authoring methodology for every Claude Code prompt, no commit entity. Middle scope, covers most of the chain but defers the planning_item-to-commit-SHA traceability piece. Defer Option III to a later release after Option II ships — rejected because the back-fill burden grows monotonically with each conversation that ships without a work_ticket record, and the bundled scope is coherent enough to ship in one workstream.
decisions.consequences: Seven planning items authored (PI-027 through PI-033). Seven design decisions named for resolution in the PI-027 methodology conversation (commit identifier strategy, granularity of resolves, ingestion trigger, commit record fields, multi-repo scoping, typed addresses kind, work_ticket authoring rule scope) plus the blocks direction question. The workstream lands as a v0.8 user-interface release per the existing release sequence (v0.7 governance entities, v0.8 code change lifecycle). After landing, every Claude Code prompt committed must be authored as a work_ticket record in the same close-out that produces the prompt file; every close-out that resolves a prior planning_item must declare the resolution in the payload; every conversation that produces commits must enumerate them in the payload for ingestion. PI-033 back-fill walks the existing 25 Open planning_items, the existing Claude Code prompt files that pre-date the methodology, and the historical commits in both repos.

decisions.title: Methodology-first sequencing for the Code Change Lifecycle workstream
decisions.context: The Code Change Lifecycle workstream involves both schema additions (new commit entity type, new relationship_kinds, refs CHECK constraint migration) and methodology changes (work_ticket authoring rule, close-out payload sections, resolution discipline). Two reasonable sequencings: schema-first (define the entity and edges, derive the methodology around the schema's affordances) or methodology-first (write the methodology document defining the seven-stage chain and the access-layer rules, then derive the minimum schema additions from the methodology).
decisions.decision: Methodology-first. PI-027 authors the methodology document at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md before any schema work begins. The methodology drives the schema spec (PI-028 commit entity schema) and the access-layer changes (PI-029). The seven deferred design decisions plus the blocks direction question are settled in the methodology conversation.
decisions.rationale: Schema-first risks designing in the dark. The seven deferred decisions are methodology questions — when does a commit count as resolving a planning item versus merely touching it? Does a conversation's commits all live in one engagement DB or split by repo? Should every kickoff document be a work_ticket or only Claude Code prompts? — that should not be answered by inspecting what the schema makes convenient. Methodology-first surfaces these as named decisions before any code lands, and the schema derives from the methodology rather than dictating to it. The cost is one drafting conversation of Doug's time; the benefit is catching second-order edges that schema-first would miss.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Schema-first — define the commit entity schema and the new relationship_kinds, then write methodology to describe how they get used. Rejected on the dark-design risk. Parallel — methodology and schema spec drafted concurrently. Rejected because the schema spec is structurally derived from the methodology and parallel drafting risks divergence. Iterative — sketch a minimum schema, run a few real conversations against it, refine. Rejected because the access-layer is constrained by migration sequencing and iterative refinement on the database structure is costly relative to a one-pass methodology document.
decisions.consequences: PI-027 (methodology) is the first planning item to resolve. PI-028 (commit schema spec) cannot begin until PI-027 ships. The methodology document at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md is the source of truth for the schema decisions; if the methodology document changes after PI-028 ships, that is a v0.8.x patch rather than a v0.9 release.

decisions.title: Code Change Lifecycle workstream decomposed into seven planning items in dependency order
decisions.context: Option III scope is substantial: methodology, schema spec, access layer plus API, close-out payload plus apply plus ingestion, UI, methodology rollout, back-fill. Two reasonable decompositions: a small number of large planning_items (e.g., three: methodology, build, back-fill) that hide intermediate progress and make granular resolution edges impossible, versus a larger number of small planning_items that increase resolution granularity at the cost of methodology overhead per planning_item.
decisions.decision: Seven planning items in strict dependency order: PI-027 methodology document; PI-028 commit entity schema spec; PI-029 schema vocab access layer and API; PI-030 close-out payload schema apply script extension and git-log ingestion tool; PI-031 UI updates for commits panel and planning_item resolution display; PI-032 methodology rollout — close-out template and work_ticket authoring rule documented; PI-033 back-fill workstream — historical work_tickets planning_item resolutions and commits.
decisions.rationale: Seven planning_items is the smallest decomposition where each has a coherent acceptance gate and corresponds to a discrete release-shippable deliverable. Three planning_items (methodology, build, back-fill) hide which schema decisions were already made versus which are pending, and offer only three resolution edges across a multi-week workstream — defeating the granular traceability the workstream is being built to provide. Twelve planning_items (one per slice of build work) is over-decomposition; the access layer and the API share infrastructure that benefits from being authored together. The seven-planning_item shape matches the v0.7 governance entity workstream's six-slice topology in spirit (one document deliverable per coherent scope) while collapsing slices that benefit from shared authoring (PI-029 covers schema migrations, vocab edits, access layer, and API together because all four touch the same files and tests).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Three planning_items (methodology, build, back-fill) — rejected for hiding intermediate state. Five planning_items (collapse PI-029 with PI-030 into one and PI-031 with PI-032 into one) — rejected because PI-029 (schema plus access plus API) and PI-030 (apply plus ingestion) have independent acceptance gates and benefit from independent resolution edges; same logic for PI-031 (UI) and PI-032 (methodology rollout). Twelve planning_items (one per implementation slice) — rejected for over-decomposition and methodology overhead. PI-033 (back-fill) as a separate workstream rather than a planning_item in this workstream — rejected because the back-fill is structurally part of the same scope and authoring it as a sibling planning_item keeps the audit chain coherent.
decisions.consequences: Seven planning items authored. Resolution order is enforced by narrative dependencies in each planning_item's description rather than by typed blocks edges, because the blocks relationship semantics in the project (direction reading of the PI-024 PI-025 PI-026 chain) was ambiguous at this conversation's authoring time and the methodology conversation (PI-027) will settle the blocks direction question in passing. The workstream's overall resolution requires all seven planning_items to reach Resolved status.

planning_items.title: Draft Code Change Lifecycle methodology document and settle deferred design decisions
planning_items.description: Author PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md defining the seven-stage code change lifecycle in V2: a planning_item motivates a planning conversation; the planning conversation produces decisions and one or more work_ticket records; an execution conversation opens against a work_ticket; the execution conversation produces commits; a close-out payload records resolves edges plus work_ticket consumptions plus commits; the apply transaction lands all of it as data; downstream queries walk the chain. Settle seven deferred design decisions plus the blocks direction question in passing. Deferred decisions are: commit identifier strategy (CM-NNN sequence with server-side auto-assignment versus SHA-as-identifier versus both); granularity of resolves edges (per-commit only via commit_resolves_planning_item, per-conversation only via conversation_resolves_planning_item, or both); commit ingestion trigger (manual command invoked by the apply script, git post-commit hook running the apply script, or background polling against git log); commit record fields (the minimum set: sha, message, author, committed_at, repository, branch, parent_sha; the optional set: files_changed_count, files_changed_paths, signed_by); multi-repo scoping (commits to the crmbuilder repo live in the CRMBUILDER engagement DB, commits to the ClevelandBusinessMentoring repo live in the CBM engagement DB — but Claude Code conversations sometimes touch both repos in a single conversation; the methodology must specify whether the conversation record itself is scoped to one engagement and the cross-repo commits are tracked via cross-engagement references, or whether the conversation forks into per-engagement child records); typed addresses relationship_kind for work_ticket-to-planning_item (define a typed kind for queryability versus continue using generic is_about); work_ticket authoring rule scope (only CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md files under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/, or all single-use kickoff documents including planning-kickoff files, or some other boundary). The blocks direction question: source blocks target meaning target waits for source, versus the inverse — settled by reading the existing PI-024 PI-025 PI-026 chain in the dogfood database and documenting the convention. Acceptance: methodology document committed; all deferred decisions recorded as decisions in the methodology conversation's close-out; kickoff prompt for the PI-028 commit entity schema conversation authored as the methodology conversation's deliverable.

planning_items.title: Author commit entity schema spec in the governance schema spec format
planning_items.description: Author PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/commit.md in the seven-section governance schema spec format established by the v0.7 schema-design workstream (purpose and position; summary; schema specification including identity, fields, lifecycle, relationships, API contract; access-layer rules; UI surface; acceptance criteria; revision control). Derives content from the methodology document at PI-027: identifier strategy per the decision chosen in PI-027, fields per PI-027 minimum and optional sets, lifecycle per PI-027 — likely append-only with one timestamp matching deposit_event's posture, relationship_kinds the entity participates in (conversation_produced_commit, commit_resolves_planning_item, commit_addresses_work_ticket if PI-027 chose per-commit granularity; possibly also commit_supersedes_commit if amends and rebases need typed handling), soft-delete posture, UI surface (commits panel — read-only browse with filter by repository, conversation, planning_item). Acceptance: spec committed at v1.0; cross-spec consistency check against the six v0.7 specs passes; identifier prefix collision check passes; the spec names the migration SQL needed for the new entity table and the refs CHECK constraint extension. PI-029 cannot begin until this spec is at v1.0.

planning_items.title: Implement commit entity schema vocab access layer and REST API
planning_items.description: Implement the commit entity end-to-end per the schema spec at PI-028 and the methodology at PI-027. Concrete deliverables: Alembic migration creating the commits table plus adding the new relationship_kinds to the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint; vocab.py edits adding commit to ENTITY_TYPES, adding the new relationship_kinds to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, and extending _kinds_for_pair with the new typed pairs; ORM model at crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/models.py; commit repository at crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/repositories/commits.py; REST API router at crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/routers/commits.py with the standard endpoint set (POST, GET list, GET by identifier, PATCH, DELETE soft-delete, GET deleted, POST restore); StorageClient methods exposing the new endpoints. Tests across access layer (approximately 10 to 15 unit tests covering CRUD, edge cases, idempotency) and API layer (approximately 5 to 10 tests covering envelope handling, filter behavior, error paths). Acceptance: full access plus api test suite green; new entity creatable via API; new relationship_kinds accepted by the cascading ReferenceCreateDialog logic; refs CHECK constraint admits new kinds. The Claude Code prompt series for this planning_item is likely structured A (migration plus vocab plus models), B (repository plus API), C (tests plus StorageClient).

planning_items.title: Extend close-out payload schema and apply script; build git-log ingestion tool
planning_items.description: Extend the close-out payload JSON schema with three new top-level sections: resolves_planning_items (mapping of PI-NNN to resolution source identifier per the DEC granularity choice in PI-027), work_tickets (array of work_ticket records authored in the same payload — closes the close-out-payloads-cannot-author-work_tickets gap surfaced in this conversation's close-out), and commits (array of commit records to be ingested as part of the apply transaction). Extend crmbuilder-v2/scripts/apply_close_out.py to process the three new sections idempotently: write resolves edges (refs rows), flip planning_item.status to Resolved on the resolved planning_items, populate planning_items.resolution_reference, POST work_ticket records via the API, POST commit records via the API, write the corresponding deposit_event references. Build a git-log ingestion tool at crmbuilder-v2/tools/ingest_commits.py that reads commits from a named branch since a baseline SHA and authors commit records via the API. The methodology in PI-027 settles whether ingestion is invoked manually by apply_close_out.py at the end of an apply, or by a git post-commit hook, or by background polling. Acceptance: close-out payload schema documented (JSON schema or markdown spec); apply script tests cover all three new sections plus idempotency on re-run; ingestion tool tested against a fixture git repo; the close-out for PI-030 itself uses the new payload sections to author its own work_tickets and commits (the methodology eating its own dog food at the moment of landing).

planning_items.title: Implement commits panel and planning_item resolution display in V2 desktop UI
planning_items.description: Implement the V2 desktop UI updates for the code change lifecycle. Commits panel under the Governance sidebar group, appended after Deposit Events per the v0.7 sidebar precedent (DEC-163): read-only browse with master/detail layout, filterable by repository, conversation, and planning_item; detail view shows the commit's edges (which work_ticket it addresses, which planning_items it resolves, which conversation produced it). Planning_items panel updates to show the resolution chain in the detail view: when a planning_item is Resolved, display the resolution_reference value plus a clickable chain back through the resolving session, the close-out payload that authored the resolves edge, the deposit_event that applied it, and the commits associated with the resolving conversation. Acceptance: panels render; UI smoke tests pass; sidebar navigation works; the planning_items panel detail view materially helps a user trace a resolved planning_item back to its delivering commits without leaving the application.

planning_items.title: Methodology rollout — close-out template and work_ticket authoring rule documented
planning_items.description: Roll the new methodology into the project's working conventions. Update PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/CLAUDE.md (or wherever the close-out methodology guide lives) with: the work_ticket authoring rule (every committed CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md is born as a work_ticket record at status ready, authored in the same close-out payload that produces the prompt file; the consuming Claude Code conversation flips it to consumed via the conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edge); the resolves discipline (every close-out that completes a prior planning_item must declare the resolution in the resolves_planning_items payload section; failure to declare leaves the planning_item Open, which is the methodology's enforcement mechanism); the commits convention (every close-out that produced commits must enumerate them in the commits payload section for ingestion). Update the close-out kickoff prompt template at the project's seed-prompt location to reflect the new sections. Update repo-level CLAUDE.md files to point at the new methodology. Acceptance: methodology guide updated; close-out template includes the new sections; one example close-out using the full new shape included in the methodology guide as a reference.

planning_items.title: Back-fill historical planning_item resolutions work_tickets and commits
planning_items.description: Retroactively populate the governance system with the historical records that pre-date the Code Change Lifecycle workstream. Three sub-tasks: (a) Walk every committed CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md file under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/ and PRDs/product/crmbuilder-automation-PRD/; for the prompts whose conversations have completed (have a corresponding apply close-out), author a work_ticket record at status consumed with the conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edge; for prompts whose conversations have not yet opened, author at status ready. The kickoff document committed in this conversation (code-change-lifecycle-workstream-establishing-kickoff.md) is back-filled here as the WT-NNN that PI-027's methodology conversation opens against. (b) Walk every Open planning_item in the CRMBUILDER engagement DB (25 planning_items at this conversation's writing) and read each against subsequent sessions to determine which should actually be Resolved; for each genuinely-resolved planning_item, author the resolves edge and populate resolution_reference. PI-021 itself is back-filled with the actual completing session identifier (currently NULL despite the Resolved status). (c) Walk git log on both repos (crmbuilder and ClevelandBusinessMentoring) and author commit records for every commit through the back-fill cutoff date, with the corresponding edges to conversations, work_tickets, and planning_items where the linkage is clear. Sub-task (b) is paired Doug-and-Claude reading per the diagnostic's finding that is_about semantics are too weak to mechanically reclassify; sub-tasks (a) and (c) can be largely scripted. Also fix the three diagnostic script bugs in tools/diagnostics/diagnose_planning_items.py (table name refs not references; unquoted PRAGMA; case-sensitive status filter) since the script will be re-run against the new schema as part of back-fill verification. Acceptance: every historical Claude Code prompt has a work_ticket record at the appropriate status; every historical resolved planning_item has a populated resolution_reference and a resolves edge; the commits table is populated for both repos through the back-fill cutoff date; the diagnostic script runs cleanly against the new schema and reports honest status distributions.
